questions.q: In which Part of the Constitution are the Fundamental Rights enshrined?
questions.explanation: Fundamental Rights are contained in Part III of the Constitution.

questions.q: What is a key feature that distinguishes Fundamental Rights from Directive Principles?
options: They cannot be claimed by citizens
They are enforceable by the courts
questions.explanation: Unlike Directive Principles, Fundamental Rights are enforceable by the courts.

questions.q: Which of the following is one of the categories of Fundamental Rights?
options: Right to Property as a fundamental right
questions.explanation: The Right to Equality is one of the recognised categories of Fundamental Rights.

questions.q: Which Fundamental Right allows citizens to approach the courts directly to enforce their rights?
questions.explanation: The Right to Constitutional Remedies lets citizens approach the courts to enforce Fundamental Rights.

questions.q: Which right ensures freedom to practise, profess and propagate religion?
questions.explanation: The Right to Freedom of Religion guarantees the freedom to practise, profess and propagate religion.

questions.q: Which category of Fundamental Rights protects minorities' right to conserve their language and culture?
questions.explanation: Cultural and Educational Rights protect minorities' right to conserve their language, script and culture.

questions.q: The Right against Exploitation chiefly prohibits which practices?
questions.explanation: The Right against Exploitation prohibits human trafficking, forced labour and similar practices.

questions.q: Fundamental Rights are guaranteed primarily against the actions of which body?
questions.explanation: Fundamental Rights are primarily guaranteed against arbitrary action by the State.

questions.q: Which Fundamental Right includes freedoms such as speech, assembly and movement?
questions.explanation: The Right to Freedom includes freedoms of speech, assembly, association, movement and residence.

questions.q: Fundamental Rights can be enforced by which courts?
options: The Supreme Court and the High Courts
questions.explanation: Fundamental Rights can be enforced through the Supreme Court and the High Courts.

questions.q: Which article guarantees equality before the law and equal protection of the laws?
questions.explanation: Article 14 guarantees equality before the law and the equal protection of the laws.

questions.q: Which article protects life and personal liberty?
questions.explanation: Article 21 protects the right to life and personal liberty.

questions.q: Which article guarantees various freedoms including freedom of speech and expression?
questions.explanation: Article 19 guarantees the six freedoms, including freedom of speech and expression.

questions.q: The Right to Constitutional Remedies, allowing citizens to move the Supreme Court directly, is found in which article?
questions.explanation: Article 32 provides the Right to Constitutional Remedies through the Supreme Court.

questions.q: Article 21A, added in 2002, made which a Fundamental Right?
options: The right to free and compulsory education for children
questions.explanation: Article 21A, added by the 86th Amendment in 2002, made free and compulsory education for children aged 6 to 14 a Fundamental Right.

questions.q: Which article abolishes untouchability and forbids its practice?
questions.explanation: Article 17 abolishes untouchability and forbids its practice in any form.

questions.q: The right to property was removed from the list of Fundamental Rights by which amendment?
questions.explanation: The 44th Amendment of 1978 removed the right to property from the Fundamental Rights, making it a legal right under Article 300A.

questions.q: Which writ is issued to produce a detained person before the court to examine the legality of detention?
questions.explanation: The writ of Habeas Corpus is used to bring a detained person before the court to test the legality of detention.

questions.q: Article 15 prohibits discrimination by the State on grounds only of religion, race, caste, sex or:
questions.explanation: Article 15 prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth.

questions.q: Article 18 abolishes which of the following?
options: Titles, except military and academic distinctions
questions.explanation: Article 18 abolishes titles, other than military and academic distinctions.

questions.q: Article 16 guarantees equality of opportunity in matters relating to:
questions.explanation: Article 16 guarantees equality of opportunity in matters of public employment.

questions.q: How many categories of Fundamental Rights are currently recognised in Part III?
questions.explanation: There are currently six categories of Fundamental Rights after the right to property was removed in 1978.

questions.q: Which writ is issued by a higher court to a lower court or authority commanding it to perform a public duty?
questions.explanation: The writ of Mandamus commands a public authority to perform a duty it has failed to perform.

questions.q: Article 23 specifically prohibits which of the following?
options: Traffic in human beings and forced labour
questions.explanation: Article 23 prohibits traffic in human beings, begar and other forms of forced labour.

questions.q: Article 24 prohibits the employment of children below the age of fourteen in which settings?
options: Factories, mines and other hazardous work
questions.explanation: Article 24 prohibits the employment of children below fourteen years in factories, mines and hazardous occupations.

questions.q: Which writ is issued to quash an order already passed by a lower court or tribunal?
questions.explanation: The writ of Certiorari is used to quash an order already passed by a lower court or tribunal.

questions.q: Which writ questions the legal authority of a person holding a public office?
questions.explanation: The writ of Quo Warranto challenges the legal right of a person to hold a public office.

questions.q: Articles 25 to 28 of the Constitution together deal with which Fundamental Right?
questions.explanation: Articles 25 to 28 set out the Right to Freedom of Religion.

questions.q: Articles 29 and 30 of the Constitution protect which Fundamental Right?
questions.explanation: Articles 29 and 30 set out the Cultural and Educational Rights, including the rights of minorities.

questions.q: Fundamental Rights available only to citizens, and not to foreigners, include the rights guaranteed under which article?
questions.explanation: The freedoms under Article 19 are available only to citizens of India, unlike Articles 14 and 21 which extend to all persons.
